name: rad-session
description:
**Make agent sessions remember each other — even across machines, even across agents (Claude + Codex).** rad-session manages a project's session lifecycle in three skills: `/startup` (per-session orientation + first-run bootstrap), `/wrapup` (per-session capture from evidence), `/add-resource` (register tools as you discover them). Built around the **canonical doc structure** aligned with published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research — the operating manual (CLAUDE.md and/or AGENTS.md per agent scope), `docs/status.md` (evidence-based reality), `docs/planning/current.md` (intent; owned by rad-planner), `docs/planning/archive/` (shipped milestone journal), `docs/decisions/` (ADRs).

What each command does:
- **`/startup`** (every session) — Fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind (Phase 0). **On first run for a project** (Phase 0.5), detects missing artifacts and bootstraps inline: stack detection, agent_scope question, operating manual Operational sections scaffold, `docs/status.md` scaffold, `.rad/profile` creation, `.claude/settings.json` / `.codex/config.toml` per scope, plugin-bloat audit. **In steady state**, reads `.rad/profile`, operating manual, `docs/status.md`, `docs/planning/current.md` and surfaces a concise briefing (<35 lines, <5s wall clock — doorman model). The bootstrap fold replaces v5.0's separate `/init` command, which conflicted with Claude Code's built-in `/init`.
- **`/wrapup`** (every session) — Refuses if no work changed since last status (Phase 0). Writes `docs/status.md` from **evidence** (git diff, test output, plan-task state) — not chat synthesis (Phase 2). Detects candidate decisions mechanically + soft triggers; mode-aware (mentor teaches and drafts an ADR; dev shows a quick list) (Phase 3). Runs cross-doc validators from rad-planner (`doc-redundancy.py` + `doc-contradiction.py`) (Phase 4). Prunes operating manual Operational sections only (sectioned-writer rule) (Phase 5). Detects milestone-shipped state → proposes archive (Phase 6). Commits + prompts to push (Phase 7). Anomaly-gated final output — silent on success (Phase 8).
- **`/add-resource`** (any time) — Register a new MCP server, CLI tool, script, or note in the operating manual's Resources section.

**Sectioned-writer rule.** The operating manual is the one file with two automated writers. rad-planner owns Constitution sections (Project, Read order, Hard boundaries, Engineering rules, Definition of done, Escalate). rad-session owns Operational sections (Commands, Compact Instructions, Claude-specific behavior, `@AGENTS.md` import line). Each plugin parses sections, modifies only what it owns, and surfaces user-added sections as preserved.

**Cross-machine continuity:** `/wrapup` auto-commits `docs/status.md`, archives, ADRs, and the operating manual; `/startup` fetches and prompts to pull. PC ↔ GitHub ↔ Laptop stays in sync. Flag overrides: `--push` / `--no-push` / `--quick` / `--non-interactive` / `--force` on wrapup; `--auto-pull` / `--no-pull` / `--bootstrap` / `--no-bootstrap` / `--agents <scope>` / `--non-interactive` / `--dry-run` on startup.

**Safety:** Never `git add -A`. Never force-pushes. Never merges or rebases automatically. Bails cleanly on diverged history. PreCompact hook fires before context compaction so state isn't silently lost.

Four Python scripts back the lifecycle: `detect-stack.py`, `detect-resources.py`, `audit-plugin-bloat.py`, `migrate-to-v5.py` (one-shot upgrade for projects on the pre-5.0 HANDOFF.md / session-log layout).

Pairs naturally with rad-planner. Works standalone — without rad-planner, `/startup` bootstrap notes missing strategic docs and `/wrapup` gracefully skips cross-doc validators if the scripts aren't present.
